what_it_does: Acceptance test for X17 that verifies Suite's R2 composition knobs (lambda, proximity/pin/semantic weights, budget, run-versions) are resolved from environment variables with the old class constants as defaults, that fresh Suite instances pick up config changes, that capabilities() exposes the live knob values, that malformed env values raise clear knob-named errors, and that the default score formula and budget cap remain unchanged.
observations: Clears all COMPANY_R2_* env vars at import to ensure an honest defaults case.
Tests both instance attributes and class-level constant access for backward compatibility.
Uses synthetic items and direct calls to private _r2_score / _r2_score_and_cap methods.
Runs without any live model, embedder, or chat/consult calls.
Validates numeric types (int for budget/versions, float for weights).
Checks that a zero semantic weight removes the semantic term entirely.
Checks that error messages name the offending env var knob.
Uses a global PASS counter and custom check/expect_raises helpers.
standouts: Proves config alters behavior without code change by constructing fresh Suite instances after env changes.
Explicitly tests fail-loud parsing with knob-named error messages.
Verifies that defaults equal the documented old constants and that class-level access still resolves to the default floor.
conventions: Preserves old class constants as the default floor.
Uses capabilities() as the registry-truth surface for reading knob values.
Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 for inbox-hygiene parity with sibling tests.
Cleans up a temporary FsStore directory in a finally block.
concerns: Mutates os.environ globally and relies on manual pop in finally blocks, so test ordering and cleanup matter.
Directly exercises private methods _r2_score and _r2_score_and_cap, coupling tightly to internal implementation.
Assumes Suite.address_tree_distance and the exact R2 formula remain stable.
Class-level default check (Suite.R2_BUDGET) could be affected by prior env pollution from other tests in the same process.
notes: An acceptance test that locks down the contract for configurable R2 composition knobs via environment variables while preserving legacy defaults and the existing scoring formula.
questions: Where in runtime.suite is the env parsing and default fallback implemented?
Does capabilities() include additional fields beyond composition_config?
How are malformed values converted into knob-named exceptions?
Do sibling suites actually rely on Suite.R2_BUDGET class-level access?
What does address_tree_distance return for addresses that are not parent/child?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to prove configurable composition, default preservation, capabilities exposure, and loud failure, and the file does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Deterministic acceptance testing without live models
intention_signals: Make R2 ranking parameters tunable by operators or a config surface without redeploying code, while keeping the previous hardcoded behavior as a safe default and exposing live values for observability.
novelty: Treats environment-driven composition knobs as a first-class feature and encodes an explicit contract covering default preservation, override behavior, capabilities exposure, and named fail-loud validation.
contribution: A deterministic, model-free acceptance test that encodes the contract for configurable R2 composition: default preservation, env-driven override, capabilities() exposure, and named fail-loud validation.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for configurable R2 composition parameters in Suite, verifying env-var overrides, default preservation, capabilities exposure, malformed-value errors, and unchanged scoring formula.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that R2 ranking weights, budget, and run-versions can be retuned via environment variables without code changes while keeping legacy defaults and behavior intact.

symbols.does: Asserts a condition, increments PASS, and prints an ok message or raises a labeled failure.
symbols.description: A lightweight test helper that records each passing assertion and fails with a descriptive label when the condition is false.

symbols.does: Expects a callable to raise an exception and records a pass if it does.
symbols.description: A helper used to verify fail-loud behavior: it fails the test if the supplied function completes without raising.

symbols.does: Constructs a fresh Suite instance over a per-test FsStore subdirectory.
symbols.description: Factory used throughout the test to create isolated Suite instances that re-read environment variables at construction time.

symbols.does: Converts a datetime to an ISO 8601 string for synthetic item timestamps.
symbols.description: A small formatting helper used to build deterministic test items with explicit recency deltas.
